meta.problem: Every morning I lose 30 minutes scanning my own repos' overnight commits, my unread security feeds, and yesterday's notes — I want one local markdown brief waiting for me instead.

agent.name: morning-brief
agent.model_rationale: Newest Opus-class — runs on the Max subscription via OAuth, so quality first; no per-run cost to weigh against.
agent.system_summary: Reads overnight git log across the user's ~/code repos, fetches their named security feeds, and skims yesterday's ~/notes; writes a single dated brief to ~/briefs/<date>.md. Runs as the user, reusing their existing logins.
agent.system_summary_note: On Claude Code (Max) this is the body of .claude/agents/morning-brief.md — frontmatter (name, model, tools) plus this prompt.
never_dos: Never push, commit, or modify any repo — read git history only
Never present a guess as a fact; mark anything unverified
Never write anywhere except ~/briefs/ — drafting only

rubric_criteria: Every repo with overnight commits gets a one-line 'what changed' summary
Each security-feed item carries a source link; unread-but-empty feeds say 'no movement'
Yesterday's notes are distilled to open threads, not re-pasted
No claim appears without a source or an explicit 'unverified' tag
outcome.judge: No built-in grader exists on Max — you build it. In-session, a judge subagent (Agent/Task tool) reads the brief plus this rubric and returns a verdict. Unattended, judge.sh runs `claude -p --json-schema` and emits {verdict, criteria:[{name,pass,evidence}], explanation}. The run/judge loop in run.sh re-runs with judge feedback up to max_iterations.

run.permission_note: Scoped allowlist plus acceptEdits — the unattended runner can read and write its brief file but is never handed a full bypass. The user did not opt into dontAsk/bypassPermissions.
run.auth_note: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is left UNSET so `claude -p` uses the Max OAuth keychain login — no API key, no per-run cost. Never run with --bare. On a 401, the token expired: `claude auth login` to refresh, or `claude setup-token` for a long-lived unattended token.
run.nested_claude_note: run.sh uses `claude -p`, which is fine because launchd runs it OUTSIDE any Claude Code session (CLAUDECODE unset). During the build session itself the agent is fired via the Agent/Task tool and graded by a judge subagent — never a nested `claude -p` inline.

mcp_servers.name: github
mcp_servers.note: Already authenticated under the user's login — no per-run credential handoff. Scope headless access with --allowedTools "mcp__github__*" if used.

connections.vault_credentials: N/A on Max — there is no vault. The agent runs AS the user and reuses already-authenticated MCP servers and local logins.
connections.github: Reuses the user's existing GitHub MCP login; the agent reads repos locally under ~/code so v0 needs no remote calls.
connections.notes: v0 reads only local repos plus public security feeds. The GitHub MCP server is listed for the roadmap (private repo activity) but is not required for the local-only v0.

files.purpose: What the agent reported yesterday — which commits and feed items it already covered — so today's brief reports changes rather than repeating itself.

memory.note: Plain local markdown the agent reads and appends to, not a CMA memory store. Add the dir with --add-dir if it lives outside the working tree.

environment.machine: Lamis's MacBook (the user's own laptop, runs as the user)
environment.add_dir_note: Anything outside the working dir the agent must read is granted with --add-dir; the home dir is not opened wholesale.
environment.networking: Inherits the machine's network — WebSearch/WebFetch reach the named public feeds; no cloud sandbox.

schedule.label: com.launch-your-agent.morning-brief
schedule.human: Every day at 6:30 AM local time, while the Mac is awake
schedule.env_note: The plist sets HOME and an explicit full PATH (~/.bun/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin — launchd does not inherit the shell PATH) and leaves ANTHROPIC_API_KEY ABSENT so OAuth keychain is used. ProgramArguments -> ["/bin/bash", "<abs path>/run.sh"]; logs to ~/Library/Logs/launch-your-agent/.
schedule.test_fire: launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.launch-your-agent.morning-brief (or just run run.sh)
schedule.laptop_closed_alternative: If the Mac is often asleep at 6:30, use a cloud /schedule routine instead — Anthropic-hosted, runs laptop-closed, counts against Max usage, ~1h min interval. See the /schedule skill.

cases.name: morning-of-may-25
cases.expected: The brief Lamis wrote by hand that morning

evals.notes: run-evals.sh loops each case through run.sh then judge.sh and records the verdict. Case 1 is the v0 input; the other two are held back as a regression check before promoting any new agent version.

defaults_applied.iteration_bound: run.sh loops run -> judge up to 3 times, re-running with judge feedback on a fail
defaults_applied.write_actions: None beyond ~/briefs/ — the agent drafts the brief, the user reads it
defaults_applied.auth: Max OAuth keychain (no API key); ANTHROPIC_API_KEY deliberately unset

interface.who: Lamis (just her, on her own machine)
interface.how: The brief lands as ~/briefs/<date>.md; she opens it with coffee. Logs in ~/Library/Logs/launch-your-agent/ for run history.

next_directions.what: Include private repo activity from GitHub, not just local clones
next_directions.why_deferred: v0 stays fully local; remote calls add a failure surface the morning brief doesn't need yet
next_directions.how: Use the already-authenticated GitHub MCP server with --allowedTools "mcp__github__*"; reference the org/repos in the agent prompt

next_directions.what: Let the brief post itself to the user's Slack instead of sitting in a file
next_directions.why_deferred: A write-action firing unattended — kept human-in-the-loop for v0
next_directions.how: Add the Slack MCP server (reused from the user's login) and gate the post behind an outbox the user clears, or run only in-session where she can approve it

next_directions.what: Run even when the laptop is closed
next_directions.why_deferred: launchd needs the Mac awake; fine while Lamis's machine is on by 6:30
next_directions.how: Move the schedule to a cloud /schedule routine (laptop-closed, counts against Max usage)

next_directions.what: Re-run the 3 eval cases before promoting any new agent version
next_directions.why_deferred: Process habit, not a build task
next_directions.how: Run evals/run-evals.sh against the new agent.md; only reload the launchd job when verdicts hold
